sanskrit:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः॥
transliteration:
patraṃ puṣpaṃ phalaṃ toyaṃ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati |
tad ahaṃ bhaktyupahṛtamaśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ ||
literal_meaning: A leaf, a flower, a fruit, water—whatever is offered to Me with devotion, that I accept, offered in love by the pure-hearted.
contextual_meaning: The psyche is reassured that the smallest, sincere gesture of care is enough to secure relational connection; grandiosity is unnecessary, authenticity is everything.
psychological_state: Secure attachment—confirmation that one's modest efforts are worthy and will not be rejected.
trigger_scenario: A client believing 'I have nothing valuable to give' learns that their simple text check-in was deeply comforting to a grieving friend.
anchor_text: Offer simple gifts with love; devotion, not size, secures connection.
